phase3_findings.description: workpad.py --rewrite-ac argparse help (and the _apply_mutations guard comment) published the stale OLD-only contract '(NEW ends with it, OLD does not)' after the guard was rebound to the resolved row. False against HEAD on the user-facing CLI surface.
A crafted multi-pair shuttle appending (post-merge) to an already-ticked [x] AC row is outside the backstop's unticked-row population and untested.
The SKILL.md coupled-invariant pin uses grep -q and matches 2 lines, so it guards wholesale removal but not single-site drift between the two coupled sentences.

fix_decisions.claim_text: crafted multi-pair shuttle onto an already-ticked [x] AC row escapes the backstop and is untested
fix_decisions.evidence: Below the configured fix threshold (important). Disclosed verbatim in the backstop's own source comment, so the published contract matches the behavior — not a documented falsehood. No gate consequence: a ticked AC already satisfies _terminal_complete_gate regardless of tag, so the shape cannot launder a deferral past the Complete gate. Revisit when _unticked_rows' row population or the pre/post delta logic is next touched.

fix_decisions.claim_text: SKILL.md coupled-invariant pin uses grep -q, matching 2 lines; not single-site-drift-proof
fix_decisions.evidence: Below threshold. The pin's purpose is the coupled-invariant guard against wholesale reversion to the OLD-only form, which it serves. A count==2 assertion would be brittle (a third legitimate mention of the phrase would turn it spuriously RED). The rendered --help pins added this iteration cover the surface that actually drifted. Revisit if the two SKILL.md sentences are ever edited independently.

fix_decisions.claim_text: state backstop residual limitations (ticked-row shuttle; net-zero remove-one/add-one)
fix_decisions.evidence: Observable fail-direction: fails toward under-blocking only on an adversarial hand-crafted multi-pair call; no wrong output, no corrupted state, no skipped gate (a ticked AC already satisfies the terminal Complete gate). Blast radius bounded and disclosed in-source. Grade supported: Suggestion.

completeness_critic.audit_population: channels that can introduce a (post-merge) AC row
completeness_critic.independent_signal: every writer of the '## Acceptance Criteria' section in workpad.py, enumerated from _find_section/_set_section_content call sites and the argparse flag surface — NOT from the guard's own pattern
completeness_critic.result: The guard's matched set is a strict subset of the independent enumeration, but every uncovered member is DISCLOSED, not silent: --replace-acs-file is named as a deliberate open limitation in the guard's scope comment, docs/implement-skill.md, and issue #338's Potential Gotchas; create/initial seeding is covered by the Phase 1.2 + Phase 3.4 classification gate rather than the workpad guard; tick flags write no AC text. No undisclosed uncovered member -> no finding. Note: this asserts the audit is a superset of one independent enumeration, not exhaustiveness.
